title: Cyclosorus toppingii
taxonomy: Cyclosorus toppingii is classified as a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns. It belongs to the genus Cyclosorus, which is a group of ferns that are native to tropical and subtropical regions. The family Thelypteridaceae is part of the larger order Polypodiales, which includes a wide range of ferns. Cyclosorus toppingii is a small to medium-sized fern, typically growing up to 1-2 meters in height. It is native to tropical regions of Asia, where it can be found growing in shaded areas and along streams.

intro: Cyclosorus toppingii, also known as Topping's fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae, native to tropical Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimalarial, and antimicrobial activities, which can aid in the treatment of fever, skin infections, and other diseases. The bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus toppingii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made with Cyclosorus toppingii include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, particularly those related to the skin and respiratory system.
